profile.sector_orientation: sector_specific — exclusively infrastructure across five verticals: energy transition, digital, transport, social, environment/circular economy
profile.origination_engine: captive_origination — sector-specialist teams in each vertical; long-established European infrastructure relationships with utilities, owners, and government concession authorities
profile.fund_type: drawdown — predominantly traditional drawdown with 10-15 year fund lives; infrastructure debt (NextGen) as complementary strategy; exploring semi-liquid for future wealth access
profile.capital_source: non_captive_capital_pool — third-party institutional; no captive insurance or balance sheet co-investment
profile.distribution_strategy: direct, consultants — institutional direct; consultant-intermediated; limited wealth-channel; exploring semi-liquid structures for future wealth access
profile.client_segment: pension_funds, swf, insurance, endowments_foundations, family_offices — European pension and insurance heavy; growing North American and Asian SWF allocators; infrastructure-dedicated mandate allocators
profile.growth_agenda: organic — fund vintage succession (Fund V, Fund VI raising); NextGen infrastructure debt as product extension; no significant M&A history
profile.share_class: single_class — Euronext Paris listed since September 2021; single class of ordinary shares
stated_strategic_priorities: Raise Fund VI at target EUR 12-15B to cement position as one of Europe's leading mid-to-large-cap infrastructure platforms; Fund V progression demonstrates LP appetite for pure-play infra exposure
Scale NextGen infrastructure debt to EUR 5B+ AUM as complementary product providing senior/subordinated lending alongside equity co-investors

Expand geographic reach in North America (New York office) and Asia-Pacific (Singapore) while maintaining core European infrastructure origination edge; explore semi-liquid products for wealth channel

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: Global infrastructure investment gap $3.5T/year (McKinsey). European infrastructure PE AUM ~$200B+. Antin EUR 33B+ AUM makes it one of largest listed pure-play infrastructure specialists.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Euronext Paris listed since September 2021. Co-founders Alain Rauscher and Mark Crosbie retain significant equity and operational leadership. Single share class.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: AMF (France) regulated. EU AIFMD-compliant. Luxembourg fund domicile. SFDR for sustainability-labeled funds. Infrastructure investments subject to sectoral regulation across European jurisdictions.
